explicit_text: On my paternal side, the family name was Peters, though it wasn't originally Portuguese. My grandfather, a Portuguese descendant, came from the Azores or Portugal and settled in Oakland. Sadly, he died in a railroad accident when I was very young, so I only remember his shocking white hair. I never knew my paternal grandmother or much about their origins beyond that.

explicit_text: Oakland was where my grandfather settled when he came from the Azores, and both my parents were born there. After my parents divorced when I was three, I lived in an orphanage in Oakland for about a year and a half before moving in with my aunt and uncle. Later, after my father remarried, we moved back to Oakland to live with him and my stepmother. Oakland is really the central place of my early family life.

explicit_text: My maternal grandfather's name was Heffron, and he came from Missouri. He and my maternal grandmother settled near what is now Petaluma, California, where they farmed. They donated some land to a school, which no longer operates, but it became public land. The Heffrons were not wealthy, but they contributed to the community in their own way.

explicit_text: My sister Norma still lives in San Francisco, and we remain very close as the only siblings left in the family. Though I grew up mostly in Oakland and other places, San Francisco is important because of my sister.

explicit_text: My mother died young in the county hospital in Alameda due to alcoholism. Alameda County is where some of the hardest moments of my early life took place, including the loss of my mother. It’s a place tied to both family tragedy and the challenges we faced growing up.

explicit_text: After my parents divorced, my father took my sister and me to Buffalo, New York, where we stayed for a time with relatives or someone on his side of the family. I remember the cold and sleeping in a loft with other children, and I even contracted scarlet fever there. Buffalo was a strange but memorable part of my childhood during a difficult period.

explicit_text: After the divorce, my father became very active in the Masonic Order, spending countless hours there while still working as a laundry truck driver. This involvement seemed to take much of his time away from home, leaving my sister and me with our stepmother. The Masonic Order was a significant part of his life during those years.
